# Notiky — LLM-Readable Product Summary

> Factual overview for AI coding agents. No marketing claims beyond what the product ships.

## What Notiky is

Notiky is the product engineering brain — the persistent context layer for teams who build software with AI agents. It is also the AI workspace where products are built, from first idea to scale.

Core primitive: a typed decision/hypothesis/insight graph with supersession, contradiction detection, and domain routing. Derivatives include ship plans, tasks, and coding-infrastructure artifacts.

Notiky is NOT an issue tracker (Linear/Jira handle execution; Notiky mirrors with context). Notiky is NOT a code editor (Cursor/Claude Code write code; Notiky feeds them product context).

## Problem it solves

AI coding tools write code fast but have zero persistent context about product decisions, customer feedback, or prior sessions. Decisions scatter across Slack, Notion, Linear tickets, and founder memory. Every new agent session starts cold.

Notiky captures the why behind every decision and serves it back via static artifacts and live MCP.

## Product cycle

```
Brainstorm → Decisions → Ship Plan → Artifacts & Tasks
     ↑___________________________________|
              context compounds
```

1. Brainstorm — AI co-founder that challenges thinking, researches, drills into specifics
2. Decisions — structured objects with provenance (who, when, why, rejected alternatives, evidence)
3. Ship plans — actionable tasks with full reasoning chains
4. Artifacts — CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, design systems, API docs

## Key features

- Decision/hypothesis graph with bi-temporal versioning and vector search (Postgres + pgvector)
- Artifact generation: static-first files regenerated from current decision state
- MCP server: live product context injected into Cursor, Claude Code, Codex mid-session
- Session capture: coding agent transcripts extracted into typed entities
- Integrations: Linear, Jira (mirror with context), Granola, Otter.ai, Google Meet
- Team workspace with domain-aware decision routing

## MCP overview

Notiky ships an MCP server (HTTP+SSE) that coding agents connect to for:

- Searching decisions and hypotheses
- Reading current artifacts for a project
- Listing contradictions and supersession chains
- Injecting session context at session start
- Capturing new decisions from agent sessions

Static artifacts (CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules) work without MCP. MCP is an enhancement for live, always-current context.

Configure via `notiky setup` and MCP token management (`notiky mcp tokens create`).

## CLI commands

```bash
brew install notiky/tap/notiky          # Homebrew install
curl -fsSL https://get.notiky.com | bash  # Install script
notiky setup                             # Configure, auth, start daemon
notiky daemon status                     # Check daemon + detected agent CLIs
notiky mcp serve                         # Serve MCP locally
notiky mcp tokens create                 # Create MCP auth token
notiky update                            # Upgrade CLI
```

Supported agent CLIs auto-detected on PATH: claude, codex, gemini, openclaw, opencode, hermes, pi.

## Tech stack

- Frontend: Next.js (App Router), React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui
- Backend: Go (Chi router, sqlc, gorilla/websocket)
- Database: PostgreSQL 17 with pgvector (768-dim embeddings)
- AI: Google Gemini (primary), Claude/GPT via OpenRouter, Vercel AI SDK
- Desktop: Electron (shared packages with web)
- Auth: BetterAuth (Google, GitHub, email OTP)

Architecture: modular monolith. Go API + shared TS packages (core, views, ui) consumed by web and desktop.

## Open vs hosted (D-009 open-core model)

Open / inspectable today:
- notiky-cli (Apache 2.0): CLI, daemon, local MCP
- notiky-brain (coming soon, Apache 2.0): schema migrations, retrieval Go library, MCP skeleton, eval harness, protocol JSON schemas

Hosted only (notiky.com):
- Consolidation orchestration (nightly RAPTOR, drift detection)
- Artifact regeneration pipeline
- Brain judge (contradiction surfacer with LLM judge)
- Orchestrator and sandbox
- Web app, desktop app, hosted MCP endpoint
- Linear integration, pre-indexed library docs corpus

- https://github.com/notiky/notiky — private (main product)
